name: geography
description: Global reference catalog wrapping `nnjeim/world`. Six entities — Country, State, City, Currency, Language, Timezone — shipped as PUBLIC read-only reference data + an authenticated + rate-limited IP→location resolver on top of MaxMind GeoLite2 with `ip-api.com` fallback. Country display names are locale-aware via `world::country.<ISO2>` for 23 locales; state / city / currency / language / timezone names remain English (vendor-limited). Every list route runs through spatie/laravel-query-builder + our response-cache middleware; entries live in Redis keyed by `(path + query + locale)` for 1h TTL. Not workspace-scoped — the catalog is global platform reference data that every marketing page, signup flow, and workspace UI depends on. `nnjeim/world` owns the countries / states / cities / currencies / languages / timezones tables + migrations + seeder (installed once via `php artisan world:install`); this module ships subclass models rebinding `config('world.models.*')` so vendor relations return our subclasses, plus the Data / Repository / Service / Controller / Policy / Provider layers that keep the rest of the app off the vendor surface.
optional_dependencies_note: Localization module (if present) receives GeolocationResolved events for Accept-Language + region-default heuristics. Geography works standalone without it.

wraps.rationale: The vendor already ships the six reference tables, the seeder, the migrations, translated country names for 23 locales, and a MaxMind GeoLite2 + ip-api.com geolocation service. Reimplementing any of it would be inventing new bugs on solved problems.
we_own: Event / Listener / Observer chain per entity
AI Tools (FindCityTool, GetLanguageTool, GetTimezoneOffsetTool, ListCountriesTool, ListCurrenciesTool)

workspace.description: Public read surface. NOT auth-gated (reference data every UI needs, including anonymous marketing + signup). Reads cached via geography.cache middleware.

platform-admin.description: Platform-admin write surface for hotfixing vendor rows (rare — vendor seeder is the source of truth; overrides survive `world:install --force`).

naming_note: The `geography::Language` entity is the global ISO-639 catalog (200+ languages seeded by nnjeim/world). It is distinct from `localization::Language`, which tracks BCP 47 tags enabled per-workspace for the translation subsystem. Both coexist under different namespaces. Callers pick based on intent: reference-catalog lookups (dropdowns, forms) → `geography::Language`; workspace locale enablement + auto-translate configuration → `localization::Language`.
not_workspace_scoped: Geography reference data is global. There is no `workspace_id` column on any of the six entities. Workspaces may express PREFERENCES via settings (default_country_code, default_currency_code, default_timezone) but never OWN reference-catalog rows.
